title: Golden Crispy Kenyan Cheese Flatbread
description: A crispy and flavorful Kenyan flatbread featuring melted cheese and a hint of bitterness, cooked to perfection in a tandoor clay pot. This dish is suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, shio cure, salt, and baking powder.
Gradually add water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a cheese slice in the center of each circle and fold the dough over to form a half circle.
Brush the tops with olive oil and sprinkle with dried oregano.
Place the flatbreads in the preheated tandoor clay pot and cook for 12 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the flatbreads from the pot and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
